speaker: charlie
text: Right, time for some sporting brilliance, and we're heading to the Pacific where the Fijian Drua have just pulled off one of the biggest upsets in Super Rugby history. They've beaten the ACT Brumbies not once but twice this season, including a shock victory in Canberra that's left the rugby world stunned.

speaker: alice
text: This is such a beautiful story, Alex. The Drua dedicated their historic win to their late president Ratu Epeli Naletikau, and their captain Mayanavanua said their thoughts and prayers go out to his family. It's sport at its most human and meaningful.

speaker: charlie
text: Absolutely. The win has completely changed the Brumbies' season outlook as they head into Super Round in Christchurch. They were on track to become the first Australian side to beat all five New Zealand teams in a regular season, but now they're vulnerable. Sometimes the underdog story is the best story of all.
